source.title: Requirement for hippocampal CA3 NMDA receptors in associative memory recall

confidence_rationale: Canonical causal demonstration that biological cued recall is genuinely partial-cue pattern completion (CA3-specific NMDA knockout selectively abolishes partial-cue recall while sparing encoding and full-cue recall). High source quality. Confidence held below 0.9 by the transfer gap: the REE bank does a single linear cosine readout, not recurrent attractor dynamics, so it inherits the behaviour but not the basin-interference failure modes.
failure_signatures: When too few of the original cues remain, completion fails outright and the correct trace is not retrieved (CA3-NR1 mutants impaired specifically in the partial-cue condition).
Place-cell representational stability silently degrades under partial-cue conditions when recurrent NMDA plasticity is absent -- the cue->trace map fails quietly rather than signalling an error.

mapping.source_claim_statement: CA3 recurrent collaterals implement an NMDA-dependent autoassociative attractor; removing CA3 NMDA receptors selectively impairs recall from partial/degraded cues while leaving encoding, full-cue recall, and CA1 place fields intact.
mapping.ree_translation: The ghost-goal bank's retrieval step (goal_match between current goal/context and a stored anchor payload) is biologically licensed to be a partial-cue completion, not an identity match: a degraded or partial current cue should still recover the full stored goal trace. This supports MECH-292's matched-retrieval premise and ARC-060's claim that a discrete addressable bank is the right structure; SD-039's stored payload is what gets completed.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The biology achieves completion via recurrent attractor dynamics with graceful degradation AND a catastrophic basin-hopping failure mode (a degraded cue can be pulled to a near-but-wrong attractor). A cosine-ranked bank reproduces graceful degradation but has no basin structure, so it neither inherits nor is protected against the biological interference pathology -- the homology is functional, not mechanistic.
mapping.source_context: Adult mice, CA3-pyramidal-cell-restricted NR1 knockout; Morris water maze + in-vivo CA1 place-cell recording; spatial allocentric memory with physical-landmark cues.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high (causal transgenic + in-vivo recording, Science, heavily replicated mechanism). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: partial-cue completion is exactly the asserted retrieval property, but 'cue' here is physical landmarks not an internal goal vector. Transfer risk moderate: attractor vs linear-readout substrate divergence.
